mechanism: Landing zone stack deploys policy exemptions against Defender for Cloud recommendation assessments. Exempted set defined in defenderForCloudExemptions.jsonc, applied uniformly. Per-recommendation selection not supported.
why: Some Defender recommendations require paid SKUs, creating unavoidable compliance-score degradation for landing zones that don't adopt those capabilities. Only recommendations requiring paid SKUs or cost-generating infrastructure qualify for the platform exemption list.

related.note: Consumption cost model defines which recommendations qualify for exemption.
Exemptions exist because remediation requires paid SKUs the team didn't opt into.
Whitelisting a resource type triggers a Defender recommendation check — new recommendations requiring paid SKUs are candidates for the exemption list.

violations: defenderForCloudExemptions.jsonc containing a recommendation that doesn't require paid SKUs.
